beats.activity: Wake up, shower, make a proper espresso at home
beats.reasoning: Cyrus refuses drip coffee and starts mornings intentionally; rainy Tuesday means a slower, deliberate wake-up. Yesterday's energy was high (50/100) but the grey forecast calls for a grounded start.

beats.activity: Commute to Georgetown job site, navigating around SR-520 closure
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound is closed 9am–noon; Cyrus leaves early (before 9am) to avoid the snarl and secure parking at the job site before rain intensifies.

beats.activity: Install solar panels on residential roof in steady rain; focus on weatherproofing and wiring
beats.reasoning: Rain runs until 2pm; Cyrus adapts by prioritizing indoor/protected work (wiring, connections) over roof positioning. The grey conditions don't stop the job, just shift tactics. Steady four-hour block fits a typical morning install.

beats.activity: Lunch break; grab a sandwich and espresso at a local Georgetown spot
beats.reasoning: Midday break to refuel; Cyrus stays in Georgetown to avoid re-crossing town during peak rain and SR-520 chaos. Espresso, not drip coffee.

beats.activity: Finish panel installation and system testing; rain clears by 2pm, allowing final roof checks
beats.reasoning: Rain ends at 2pm; Cyrus uses the afternoon window to complete rooftop work, verify connections, and ensure the system is weather-sealed. Nearly four-hour afternoon block rounds out a full day.

beats.activity: Drive home; SR-520 closure lifted, but evening traffic still heavy
beats.reasoning: Commute home after a long work day; 50 min to account for lingering post-closure congestion on cross-lake routes. Tired but satisfied.

beats.activity: Quick shower, change clothes, prepare for evening
beats.reasoning: Post-work cleanup and rest; Cyrus is tired from a wet day on the roof. Prepares for evening leisure.

beats.activity: Salsa dancing class at a studio in Ballard
beats.reasoning: Rainy, low-energy day calls for a mood lift; salsa dancing is a core passion. Evening class gives Cyrus a chance to unwind and connect with the community. Commute is ~20 min from Shoreline.
Return home after class; ~25 min drive accounts for evening traffic and post-class wind-down mindset.

diary: Started the day right with a proper espresso at home—none of that rushed coffee-shop nonsense. The commute was a mess thanks to the SR-520 closure, but I knew the back roads. Georgetown job site was wet and miserable all morning, rain coming down steady while I'm up on someone's roof making sure every connection is sealed tight. Can't have water getting into the electrical work. Grabbed a sandwich at Molly Moon's around one and actually felt human again. By afternoon the rain cleared out, which made the final testing go smooth. Drove home in that heavy post-closure traffic, but honestly it gave me time to decompress. Cleaned up quick and headed over to Velocidad Latina for salsa class—needed to move, needed the music and the energy. My hips remember how to do this. Left feeling loose and alive.
thought_process: The weather was a real factor today—I had to rethink my usual roof work rhythm with that steady rain. Safety first, always. The SR-520 closure threw off the morning, but I've lived here long enough to know the workarounds. Work felt solid and purposeful, which kept my mood steady even in the drizzle. I think I needed that physical outlet tonight more than I realized; after hours on a roof in crappy weather, my body was craving movement that wasn't about installation brackets and wire management. Salsa scratched that itch perfectly. The drive home was long but gave me space to transition from work-mode to the evening. Feeling satisfied but tired now.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
